challenge: Standards designed around conventional vehicle controls
current_condition: Some Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Standards contain requirements developed for vehicles with manual controls. NHTSA has announced rulemakings addressing ADS-equipped vehicles without those controls.
consumer_effect: Purpose-built autonomous vehicles may need exemptions or additional equipment while permanent standards are updated.
recommendation: Modernize vehicle safety standards using technology-neutral performance requirements.

current_condition: Manufacturers may use temporary Part 555 exemptions while permanent standards are updated, but the statutory pathway limits the number of exempt vehicles that may be sold annually.
consumer_effect: Limited exemption volumes may make large-scale deployment and manufacturing more difficult.
recommendation: Modernize the exemption pathway while preserving evidence-based safety review.

current_condition: Federal AV policy currently relies on existing agency authorities, exemptions, rulemakings, guidance, and state law rather than one comprehensive act of Congress.
consumer_effect: Developers and consumers may face uncertainty as responsibilities remain divided across agencies and jurisdictions.
recommendation: Create clear federal legislation defining national standards and the responsibilities of federal and state governments.

current_condition: Many Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations were written around vehicles with human drivers. FMCSA has sought input on which requirements may need to be amended, revised, or removed for ADS-equipped commercial vehicles.
consumer_effect: Unclear requirements may delay freight deployment
recommendation: Modernize commercial motor vehicle rules for automated operation

current_condition: Transit automation projects may face institutional, policy, procurement, funding, and regulatory barriers that differ from private passenger-vehicle deployment.
consumer_effect: Public agencies may face additional approval complexity
recommendation: Create clear guidance for autonomous transit pilots and procurement

current_condition: AV deployment interacts with roadway infrastructure, work-zone information, traffic-control devices, and access to consistent traffic-law data.
consumer_effect: Inconsistent infrastructure conditions may restrict operating areas
recommendation: Develop interoperable infrastructure guidance without mandating one technology

current_condition: Some connected transportation applications rely on V2X communications and access to the 5.9 GHz spectrum. These issues affect connected safety services but are not required for every autonomous driving system.
consumer_effect: Unclear spectrum or interoperability rules may limit some connected safety and infrastructure services.
